title: ephedra major subsp. procera
intro: Ephedra major subsp. procera, also known as the Mormon tea or joint fir, is a species of flowering plant in the cactus family, native to North America. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly as a decongestant and bronchodilator, due to the presence of ephedrine and pseudoephedrine alkaloids. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as asthma and allergies, as well as its potential to aid in weight loss and improve mental alertness. Ephedra major subsp. procera is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, capsules, and extracts, but its use should be carefully regulated due to potential side effects and interactions with other medications.
